name: Cleveland-Cliffs Inc.

profile.description: Cleveland-Cliffs is the largest flat-rolled steel producer in North America with 25,000 employees. Vertically integrated from iron ore mining through steelmaking. Revenue grew 6.3% YoY.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): Revenue grew 6.3% with vertical integration strategy from iron ore to finished steel. AI Deployment (0/2): No AI products. Market Share (2/2): Largest flat-rolled steel producer in North America. Product Pipeline (0/2): Incremental capacity. R&D Intensity (1/1): Metallurgy R&D.

inspiration.label: WEAK
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (1/4): Standard steelmaker mission. Community Impact (1/2): Rust Belt community employer. Employee Retention (1/2): 25,000 employees with union workforce. Insider Buying (0/2): No data.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Revenue grew 6.3% but -6.44% margin and -18.60% ROE reflect steel cycle trough [1]. D/E of 129.32% is elevated. Forward P/E of 32.88 above target but PEG of 0.43 attractive.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): Physical steelmaking immune to AI. AI data centers increase steel demand for construction. AI Opportunity (0/1): No AI revenue.

infinite_game.summary: Cleveland-Cliffs under CEO Lourenco Goncalves operates with aggressive, competitive finite game mindset focused on market share battles and confrontational labor/political tactics.

investment_thesis: Cleveland-Cliffs is the largest N.A. flat-rolled steel producer with PEG of 0.43, offering cyclical recovery value [1].
thesis_continued: Margin of -6.44%, ROE of -18.60%, and D/E of 129.32% reflect steel cycle trough and acquisition leverage [1].
key_catalysts: US infrastructure spending driving steel demand
key_risks: Import competition from Asian steel producers
High leverage at 129.32% D/E in a cyclical industry
sources: Yahoo Finance — CLF Key Statistics, accessed June 2026
